title: Seven Tiny Packages
synopsis: When Theo's invention to help animals starts working in unexpected ways, he follows clues through a corn maze at dusk, discovering that seven mysterious gifts hold the key to understanding what his machine has really done.

pages.text: A tiny package sits on my windowsill. No one put it there. I check the lock. Still locked. The package is wrapped in green paper. It has my name on it. The handwriting looks like chicken scratches. I unwrap it carefully. Inside is one sunflower seed. Just one. That's strange. I put the seed in my pocket. Then I hear voices outside. They sound far away. Like they're calling from the old corn maze behind our house.

illustration.description: Theo, a boy with short curly blonde hair, light skin, and round blue eyes, sits on his bed holding a tiny green package. Sunlight streams through the window. His expression is puzzled and curious. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with candy bright colors, squash and stretch proportions, and comedic poses with rubber hose animation feel.
illustration.alt: Theo examining a small green package while sitting on his bed

pages.text: I built something last week. A machine to help animals. It was supposed to make food appear for hungry creatures. I tested it once. Then it broke. At least I thought it broke. Now I'm not so sure. The voices from the corn maze sound happy. They sound grateful. I grab my notebook. Time to investigate.

illustration.description: Theo, a boy with short curly blonde hair, light skin, and round blue eyes, stands next to a makeshift contraption made of cardboard boxes, tubes, and blinking lights. His round blue eyes are wide with realization. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with bouncy dynamic energy, oversized expressions, and slapstick energy.
illustration.alt: Theo standing next to his homemade invention with a surprised expression

pages.text: Tuesday brings package number two. Wednesday brings number three. Thursday, Friday, Saturday - more packages arrive. Each one appears on my windowsill at sunrise. Each one contains something different. An acorn. A berry. A small twig. Tiny pieces of corn. A flower petal. The voices from the maze get louder each day. More excited. I write down everything I notice. The packages smell like fresh dirt. They're warm to touch.

illustration.description: Theo, a boy with short curly blonde hair, light skin, and round blue eyes, sits at a desk with six tiny packages lined up in front of him. He's writing in a notebook with intense concentration. Each package is a different color. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with candy bright colors and comedic poses showing his detective-like focus.
illustration.alt: Theo taking notes while examining six small packages on his desk

pages.text: The corn maze has been here for years. Farmer Jenkins planted it when I was little. But this year something's different. I can hear rustling sounds. Squeaking sounds. Chirping sounds. Animals live in there now. Lots of animals. The maze has become their home. I think my machine worked after all. But not the way I planned.

illustration.description: Theo, a boy with short curly blonde hair, light skin, and round blue eyes, peers through binoculars toward a tall corn maze in the distance. Various animal silhouettes can be seen moving between the corn stalks. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with bouncy dynamic movement and oversized expressions showing his amazement.
illustration.alt: Theo looking through binoculars at a corn maze with animal shadows visible

pages.text: Sunday evening arrives with package number seven. This one is different. It contains a map. A map of the corn maze. There's a red X marked in the center. The sun is setting. Golden light makes the corn stalks glow. I hear my name being called from inside the maze. Time to solve this mystery. I stuff all seven gifts in my backpack. My notebook goes in too.

illustration.description: Theo, a boy with short curly blonde hair, light skin, and round blue eyes, stands at the entrance to a towering corn maze at dusk. He holds a small map in one hand. Golden sunset light streams between the stalks. His expression shows determination mixed with nervousness. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with candy bright colors and dramatic lighting creating a comedic adventure feel.
illustration.alt: Theo at the corn maze entrance holding a map during golden hour

pages.text: The maze is alive with sounds. Footsteps scurry past. Wings flutter overhead. Something chittering runs between my feet. I follow the map carefully. Left turn. Right turn. Straight ahead. The corn is taller than me. Much taller. It blocks out the sky. But I can still hear the voices. They're getting closer. 'This way!' someone squeaks. 'Over here!' chirps another voice.

illustration.description: Theo, a boy with short curly blonde hair, light skin, and round blue eyes, walks through narrow corridors between towering corn stalks. Small animal shapes dart around his feet and fly overhead. His eyes are wide with wonder and slight worry. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with squash and stretch proportions, bouncy dynamic energy, and rubber hose animation feel.
illustration.alt: Theo walking through corn maze corridors with animals scurrying around

pages.text: The center of the maze opens up like a secret room. Animals everywhere. Squirrels. Rabbits. Field mice. A family of raccoons. Two owls. Even a small deer. They all turn to look at me. Their eyes are bright and friendly. In the middle sits my machine. It's working perfectly. But it's not making food appear. It's doing something better. It's calling the animals home. Giving them a safe place to live.

illustration.description: Theo, a boy with short curly blonde hair, light skin, and round blue eyes, stands in a circular clearing surrounded by various forest animals - squirrels, rabbits, raccoons, owls, and a deer. His invention sits in the center, glowing softly. All the animals look happy and peaceful. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with candy bright colors, oversized expressions of joy, and bouncy dynamic energy.
illustration.alt: Theo in maze center surrounded by happy animals with his glowing invention

pages.text: A small rabbit hops forward. She's holding something in her mouth. Another tiny package. The seventh gift wasn't the last one. I unwrap this new present carefully. Inside is a thank-you note. Written in those same chicken scratch letters. 'Your machine saved our homes,' it says. 'The corn maze is perfect for us. Thank you for caring about animals.' I smile. My invention worked exactly right. Just not how I expected. The best surprises never do.

illustration.description: Theo, a boy with short curly blonde hair, light skin, and round blue eyes, kneels down reading a tiny note while a small rabbit sits nearby. All the animals gather around him in a circle. Everyone looks happy and content. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with candy bright colors, comedic poses showing warmth and friendship, and slapstick energy in the happy expressions.
illustration.alt: Theo reading thank-you note surrounded by grateful animals in the maze

metadata.moralLesson: Sometimes our efforts to help create wonderful results in ways we never imagined.
discussionQuestions: How did Theo feel when his invention didn't work the way he planned?
What clues helped Theo solve the mystery?
Why was the corn maze a good home for the animals?

coverPrompt.description: Theo, a boy with short curly blonde hair, light skin, and round blue eyes, holds a tiny wrapped package while standing at the entrance of a towering corn maze. Golden sunset light streams between the corn stalks. Six more small packages float mysteriously in the air around him. The art style is exaggerated cartoon with bouncy dynamic energy, candy bright colors, and comedic poses.
coverPrompt.alt: Theo examining a small package at a corn maze entrance with floating gifts around him

questions.question: What was in the first package Theo received?
questions.explanation: The first package contained one sunflower seed, which Theo found strange.

questions.question: Where did Theo hear voices calling from?
questions.explanation: Theo heard voices calling from the old corn maze behind his house.

questions.question: How many packages did Theo receive in total?
questions.explanation: Theo received seven packages over the week, plus one more from the rabbit, making eight total.

questions.question: What did Theo's invention really do?
choices: Called animals to a safe home
questions.explanation: Theo's invention called animals to the corn maze, giving them a safe place to live.

questions.question: Who gave Theo the final thank-you note?
questions.explanation: A small rabbit brought Theo the final package containing a thank-you note.
